entry.title: Is it possible to read XLSX files without Microsoft Excel?
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm a beginner in SAP ABAP and I need help. The FM ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE requires a Microsoft Excel to be accessible in the system or else the function module will return sy-subrc 2, UPLOAD_OLE. Despite downloading an alternative application that can access the XLSX Document, e.g. WPS Office Sheets, it's still not working.

I've heard from several solutions which is using abap2xlsx, but I was wondering all of the other solutions other than this. Is there any alternative way to solve this issue? Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Future Dated Hires Provisioning from SF EC/Onboarding to Azure AD / Onprem AD
entry.content: @vslavova1 read you comment once more, which attributes are you populating cn from? We had som issues where we used Custom03 to create CN - and Custom03 wasn't synchronized before employees startDate = today

entry.title: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 23 - Get your goods ready for a trip with the SAP Route!
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog post for the SAP EWM Podcast Episode 23 – Get your goods ready for a trip with the SAP Route!

You can listen to the podcast episode in English and in German on Spotify, Amazon Music, Apple Podcasts or Sway! Enjoy!

The idea of a route! 

A route basically describes a defined path of a journey or of a trip. When companies ship goods, they also send them on a journey. To make this journey as efficient, cost saving, safe and fast as possible it is necessary to find the right route.

This blog post is about the route in SAP and which processes are affected by the route. We also discuss the differences between routes in SAP ERP and SAP EWM and about the integration of SAP Transportation Management. 

The Route in SAP ERP

Let’s first clarify the idea of a route or route determination in SAP. The route is about clarifying what is to be transported - how - where from and where to.

Let's make an example: We get a customer order and the customer wants to have the goods tomorrow. This means that the goods must be picked and shipped promptly. It is necessary to create an express order in our SAP system with a corresponding shipping condition. Let's pick up on the four questions again:

What - this are the ordered goods that have transport requirements due to their characteristics - e.g. bulky goods can only be transported by freight forwarder.
How - this describes the shipping condition, e.g. “by twelve o'clock tomorrow” or in other words - express delivery.
Where from - our warehouse.
Where to - this is the customer's shipping address.

Define Route in SAP S/4HANA

 Based on these information, we can now start planning the journey for our goods that means we can determine a route in SAP based on the master data and defined rules. 

Route determination in Sales Orders

The transportation costs are then also calculated from the route - we all know this when we privately select “Express shipping” in the webshop, for example. In this case, the transportation costs can be determined directly from the route. The route also determines the means of transport and the involved stages. It also influences the order scheduling that means when can we deliver with regard to how long does it take to transport the goods to their destination. This transportation lead time can be used to carry out backward scheduling to determine when goods need to be loaded in order to meet the desired delivery date.

Transportation and Delivery Scheduling in S/4HANA based on route information

Different sales order items can also have different routes if, for example, bulky goods and small parts are in one order but it could be that we send them out differently for example the small parts with a parcel service provider and the bulky goods with a freight forwarder. 

It is important to say that the route is not real transportation planning, as the route is only determined at item level and does not carry out any cross-item or cross-order planning. That only happens during transportation planning.

Let's have a closer look to important terms which were already mentioned: 

Shipping condition -  describes the “General shipping strategy for goods to deliver them from the supplier to the customer for example ‘Standard’ or ‘Express shipping’ strategy. The shipping condition can  be maintained directly in the customer master data and is then used for sales order creation or it can be linked to the order type.

Shipping type - specifies the means of transport used for transportation for example ship by truck, rail, ship or plane.

Transportation group - summarizes the properties that are important for shipping the goods. For example, it can indicate properties such as “liquid” or “to be transported cooled”. The transportation group is also maintained in the material master since it describes material characteristics.

Means of transport - describes a grouping of resources for example cool-trucks, 40-ton trucks or 20-feet containers.

Means of transport type - groups together different means of transport that are of the same type for example all available trucks are assigned to the means of transport type “Truck” which can be a 40-ton truck or a cooled truck.

Route determination for sales order items uses order header data and material master data. The shipping country and the destination country or the corresponding regions as well as the shipping condition come from the order header. The transportation group is taken from the material master of the corresponding item. As mentioned before customers could have different priorities for different sales order items and therefore different shipping conditions can be relevant and it is also possible to determine different routes for different order items.

During outbound delivery creation a new route determination takes place. And this determination also considers the delivery weight in addition. Because the delivery contains all the goods which are shipped together and therefore the relevant shipping weight is now available and can influence of course also the route determination.

Route determination in SAP S/4HANA for outbound deliveries

The route in the outbound delivery is now a header information. That means that there is at least a separate outbound delivery for each route. The delivery then uses the characteristics of the route like transit time or transportation lead time to plan the shipping.

There are also many companies who work with regional freight forwarders. The route in SAP ERM is often  used in that case to group deliveries according to regions. Companies then define, for example, the northern route, the west route, the south route and so on. If, for example, a shipper uses a specific forwarder for domestic transport from its distribution center to all cities in the western region, the shipper can define receiving zones for route determination for example all cities in US that begin with zip code 9. The receiving zones can then be used to find specific routes and the corresponding freight forwarders via the route.

Everything we have discussed so far is known as “SD route” or “LE-TRA route”. Both terms are used, but they mean the same SAP object!

Route and route determination in SAP S/4HANA

 The SCM-Route in SAP EWM

First of all: Route determination in EWM is independent of route determination in ERP! That means there are different determination frameworks, different route objects and there is also no distribution of the ERP master data SD route to EWM! The Supply Chain Management route (SCM-Route) in EWM is a completely independent object! And it can of course make sense to carry out another route determination in the warehouse management system, that means in EWM. Just think about the weight or quantity which may have changed because certain products cannot be delivered as desired.  

But EWM can also work with the SD route. The SD route is send to EWM within the outbound delivery and additional information such as the times from scheduling as “planned goods issue “ is also available in the EWM outbound delivery. Customers can decide via configuration in EWM which route they want to use.

SCM Route in SAP EWM

 How is the SCM-Route determined in EWM?

The SCM route is determined with the so called “Routing Guide”! The determination logic is very similar to ERP. The routing guide uses:

  • Ship-from location and Ship-to location
  • Shipping condition
  • Transportation group

This is more or less the same as for the SD route determination.

In addition, the determination in EWM can check whether the SCM-Route is suitable for dangerous goods. Therefore the dangerous goods master data in EH&S is used. There are also other criteria that can be taken into account for the determination:

  • Minimum or maximum weight or volume per item or per delivery
  • Maximum length/width/height of the products

The SCM-Route determination is therefore more granular than the SD-Route determination and therefore it allows a more specific planning. In addition, transportation costs can be taken into account in the determination that means the most cost-effective route can be determined.

Route Determination in SAP EWM with SCM Routing Guide

The determination takes place automatically when the EWM outbound delivery order is created and it can also be manually triggered later. In EWM there is only one route field in the EWM delivery order header, so there can only be one route and customer can decide via configuration if they want to use the SD-Route or the SCM-Route.

What is the route actually used for in EWM – independent if it’s the SD or SCM route?

The route can be used for example for wave determination. All deliveries of certain routes can be grouped into the same waves. And the route also influences scheduling, because the route is used to determine the time at which the delivery should leave the warehouse for example via the departure calendar of the SCM Route. 

The route can also be used in EWM to plan EWM transportation units. For this purpose, there is a report in EWM where the route can be used as a main selection criteria.

The route can also be used in consolidation group determination, e.g. to determine which deliveries can be packed together. And the route can also be taken into account in packaging planning and the route can then be used to determine special work centers as part of work center determination. The creation of pick orders can also be influenced by the route. 

Another important point is staging zone determination, which can be influenced by the route. This allows deliveries on certain routes to be directed to specially designated zones. The creation of an invoice before goods issue can also be activated for certain routes. And in loading, the route can be used to check whether unplanned loading is permitted on an existing shipment in EWM.

Example:  Let's assume we have received a customer order and we have determined a route that specifies shipping via a certain parcel service provider and tomorrow is the desired delivery date. We have swap bodies at dedicated warehouse doors for this parcel service provider which are picked up by the service provider at fixed times.

Based on this information we can find an appropriate wave via the route and the planned goods issue time of the delivery item ensures that we find a wave which is picked, packed, staged and loaded according to the desired delivery date. After picking, the packages are moved to workstations that are specially equipped for parcel shipping. The appropriate staging zone and the door where the swap bodies are waiting are determined based on the route. All determinations happen completely automatically in the background and ensure a smooth delivery in time. 

It is easy to see that the route is an object that has major influence on the shipping processes in SAP EWM. The route in EWM takes over dispatch control if no explicit transportation planning takes place.

SAP EWM uses the SCM route since the beginning. However, in 2016 it was not initially adopted into EWM on S/4HANA. Since the release S/4HANA 2021, the SCM Route is now also available in EWM on S/4HANA - both embedded and decentralized.

The SCM route is also used as part of the Global ATP (Available-to-Promise), which takes place in SAP APO. It provides information on transportation and therefore enables a more precise delivery date commitment. And that is why we talk about the SCM route and not the EWM route, because it is historically an SCM object and is still used by other SCM modules too.

Transportation Cross-Docking is based on the SCM-Route 

Transportation cross-docking is one of the planned cross-docking scenarios in SAP EWM. With transportation cross docking, the ordered goods do not go directly from the initial warehouse to the end customer, but they are routed via one or more intermediate locations without having to be repacked there. Reason for the process may be, for example, that several deliveries are consolidated, the goods are processed centrally for export or the means of transportation is simply changed.

In case of a customer order, the initial EWM managed warehouse which ships the goods receives the outbound delivery to the customer. EWM then carries out a route determination and EWM finds a SCM-Route which is transportation cross-docking relevant and together with the route an intermediate warehouse location is determined - the so-called cross-docking warehouse.

The location of the interim warehouse becomes the actual ship-to-party of the outbound delivery in EWM and the original final customer becomes the final-ship-to-party of the delivery. Follow-up documents for cross-docking are then automatically created in SAP ERP with the goods issue posting of the initial outbound delivery. The follow up documents are an inbound delivery for the cross-docking warehouse and an outbound delivery from the cross-docking warehouse to the final goods recipient. Both deliveries know each other and they are distributed as a delivery couple to the EWM-managed cross-docking warehouse. These special deliveries are marked as Cross Docking delivers and they can trigger special cross-docking or consolidation processes in EWM.

There is also a separate transportation cross-docking monitor in SAP ERP that you can use to monitor the progress of the cross-docking process. Transportation cross-docking is not only for sales orders but can also be used for stock transport orders too. The trigger is the SCM route determination in the initial warehouse which has to be managed by SAP EWM.

How do the route and transport planning fit together?

At SAP there are two solutions that we can use to plan and manage transportation. SAP Transportation Management and LE-TRA. LE-TRA stands for Logistics Execution Transportation.

SAP Transportation Management is SAP's strategic solution for this topic. LE-TRA as compatibility scope in S/4HANA can only be used in SAP S/4HANA until the end of 2030. The SD-Route or LE-TRA-Route in ERP is not affected by the end of the usage rights and can be used in order documents and delivery documents even after 2030! (see SAP Note 2270199)

Shipment planning with LE-TRA

If LE-TRA is used, the route can be used for example as a selection criteria during shipment creation. Transportation planners can select all non-assigned outbound deliveries with a specific route to combine them into one shipment. Using the LE-TRA shipment as a bracket for outbound deliveries in order to process them together, print delivery documents etc. is certainly one of the most common use cases of SAP customers.

The route can then be transferred from the deliveries to the shipment header as shipment route (LE-TRA-Route). As mentioned at the beginning, the route also defines the means of transport for example by truck, rail, ship or plane. - and the route can be also be used for transportation scheduling that means how long will the shipment take including administrative effort and so on. And the route can also be used to calculate transportation costs for the shipment.

Transportation Management with SAP TM

In SAP TM the route object no longer exists. In TM there is the so-called “Standard or default route”, but this has nothing to do with the SD-Route or SCM-Route. It’s only a simplification of transportation planning in TM: In short, the simplification is - that the optimizer does not always have to be triggered to perform simple planning. Among other things, the default route is used for sales order scheduling for example.

Sales order scheduling supports the delivery date determination in the ERP sales order. It is comparable to the determination via the SD-Route. However sales order scheduling uses information from SAP Transportation Management which offers for example a better forecast of the required transportation times or it is necessary for customers who only use SAP TM and no SD-Routes for example. 

Sales order scheduling with SAP TM is based on strong communication between SAP ERP and SAP TM. First, backward scheduling is performed in SAP TM starting from the requested delivery date in order to determine the pick date or availability date of the goods. If the availability check (the ATP in ERP) cannot confirm this date, SAP TM now determines a new possible delivery date via forward scheduling - based on the committed material availability.

This feature shows perfectly the strength of SAP: the deep and native integration of different SAP modules. In this case SAP Sales Distribution, the available to promise and SAP Transportation management in order to determine the most reliable delivery promise for customers.

As mentioned above SAP TM follows a different approach compared to LE-TRA and it works with locations, transportation zones, stages and so on. And the various transportation demands or freight units are then turned into one or more freight orders with start and destination locations in TM, which are approached in a specific sequence. This planning can be done manually or with the help of the TM optimizer. There are also schedules and much much more in SAP TM. As you can see from the explanation, the route no longer plays a role in TM. 

The planning results are for example freight orders with start and destination locations. These freight orders are the objects which are used for the TM-EWM integration. This means that SAP EWM does not receive any further routes from the TM system but rather a freight order with assigned delivery items, time information, carrier information, loading sequence and so on. 

What are the consequences for warehouse planning and operations?

If a freight order exists in SAP TM and this freight order is send to the warehouse, EWM can now control many processes based on the freight order instead of using the route. Processes like wave determination, warehouse operations scheduling or staging zone and door determination can be done with regard to the transport instead of the route.

And this is independent of the used integration scenario between EWM and SAP TM – that means it works with the classic EWM-TM integration using Transportation Units and also with Advanced Shipping and Receiving where EWM works directly on the TM freight order.

Conclusion

The route in SAP Logistics is an important tool to control shipping processes in SAP ERP as well as in SAP EWM. With the native integration of SAP Transportation Management and SAP Extended Warehouse Management there are new possibilities to manage the shipping of goods based on freight orders. 

Please visit our SAP EWM Podcast Landing Page with all Episodes, Blog Posts and Podcast Notes!

entry.title: RAP OData v4 Web-API metadata set nullable option
entry.content:
I implemented an OData v4 Web-API and realized that the metadata document contains nullable="false" for almost every property (all but Edm.Date typed properties) of my entities. As far as I recall the metadata document of the RAP implementation (UI service) of the S4D437 course didn't contain that much nullables. I searched for an answer and found Andre Fischers post in this thread that says that you can't set it directly but influence it by the implementation.

Sadly there is nothing mentioned "how" you influence it. What do I have to do to make properties nullable?

entry.title: Re: Create New Controlling Area in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: If different countries use different Charts of Accounts (CoA) in one tenant, the Controlling Area needs to be separated. In this case, do we need to split the tenant?

entry.title: No output parameters could be determined for document 7000000011
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I have already created the Supplier Quotation and Purchase Requisition configuration. But in Manage RFQ, following the Document Type is blank. Am I missing any configuration?

1. Blank Document 

2. Warning message after save RFQ Document 

3. This makes me unable to create a Supplier Quotation 

 

Please guide me. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS View Service Binding unpublished after transport
entry.content: I am in an SAP public cloud environment ， hot to use transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and activate

entry.title: Scrap in production confirmation
entry.content:
Dear PP experts,

I have a question about the standard best practice related to Scrap in Production Confirmation.

In my example, I have a production order quantity with 3PC and there is a three operation step in my production order.

First operation, I posted confirmation with yield = 3

Second Operation, I posted confirmation with yield = 2 and scrap = 1

Third Operation, I posted confirmation with yield = 2

After these confirmation, I'm able to post GR for production order with quantity = 2 based on the example production order.

 


In my understanding, I posted scrap quantity for 1PC for second operation and post GR for yield quantity =2 after that the production order become completed and status should be changed automatically to complete delivered in SAP system and scrap quantity is reflected to production order.

But in my partner system, the status is still left partially delivered and scrap quantity is not reflected in the production order data.

This calculation and automatic status decision logic is not working SAP Public Cloud, or Is it possible to update the status as a completed with the scrap quantity based on configuration?

Kindly helping to solve this issue,

Best Regards

entry.title: Unable to Access Security Page in SAP Datasphere Trial Account for User Management
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently using an SAP Datasphere trial account while following the course "Introduction to SAP Datasphere Administration".

The issue I encountered is as follows:
- The trial account does not allow me to access the Security page under Administration.
- I am unable to view, create, or manage users because my assigned user lacks the necessary roles.
- This issue prevents me from completing the "Add Users and Assign Roles in SAP Datasphere" section of the course.

Could you please assist how to enable the required roles or providing guidance to resolve this issue?

entry.title: I How to extract the BP address information?
entry.content:
Dear All.

We need to do I/F about address(Postal code, address, etc.) to other system.

But I think CDS View of BP address(I_BusinessPartnerAddressTP) is not release.

In this case, Please let me know how to extract the BP address information.

Also, If there is same case of other requirement in the future, please let me know how to extract too.

Best Regards,

Yuki Tsumura

entry.title: Re: Error While Publishing V4 version service with RAP
entry.content: I am in an SAP public cloud environment ， hot to use transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and activate

entry.title: How to add attachement to Message?
entry.content:
Hi all, we currently have an interface between SAP and Salesforce on BTP. When we have wrong or missing data, the user in salesforce gets the same error message as its shown in CPI Monitoring Status:

Error Details
com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: HTTP operation failed invoking https://XXXondemand.com/odata/SSCO/SF_BPARTNER_DATA_SRV/HeaderSet with statusCode: 500. The MPL ID for the failed message is : XXX
 
In CPI we have 3 attachments to each error, two headers, one body: 
HTTP_Receiver_Adapter_Response_Body
 
I want to extract the text of the body (the message we get from SAP) and add it to the status (which is also the notification in salesforce). Here is a text what data is wrong, so the user could correct it directly. We have two groovy scripts. Could anyone please help me to do so? I tried with GPT but all we could do is: "An internal server error occured: No signature of method: com.sap.it.op.agent.mpl.factory.impl.MessageLogImpl.getAttachment() is applicable for argument types: (java.lang.String) values: [ErrorBody]."
Unfortunatly the extraction of the attachment didnt work.
 
I will paste the original codes, without any transformation.

 

//* Groovy Error Exception Handling

package com.scco.crm
 
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.msglog.MessageLog
import com.sap.it.api.msglog.MessageLogFactory
 
import groovy.json.JsonBuilder
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper
 
import java.util.concurrent.ThreadLocalRandom
import java.util.HashMap
 
import org.apache.camel.CamelException;
import org.apache.camel.component.ahc.AhcOperationFailedException
import org.apache.camel.component.cxf.CxfOperationException
 
/**
* Builds a general error response for the CRM-ERP integration APIs.
* 
* @Param message the iFlow's Message instance.
* @return the possibly modified iFlow's Message instance.
*/
def Message processData(Message message) {
	MessageLog messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
 
	String body = message.getBody(String.class)
	messageLog.addAttachmentAsString('LastResponseBody', body != null ? body : '(null)', "text/plain")
	String errorBody = null // the ordinary body may not be the error body, we derive the error body later
	Map<String,String> response = new HashMap<>()
	Map<String,String> fields = new HashMap<>()
	int internalStatusCode = 0
	Map<String, Object> map = message.getProperties();
	def ex = map.get("CamelExceptionCaught");
	if (ex instanceof Exception) {
		String exClassName = ex.getClass().getName()
		messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('CamelExceptionCaught', exClassName)
		response.put("cause", exClassName)
		if (isSpecificCamelException(ex, messageLog)) {
			if (isNoneEmpty(ex.getResponseBody())) {
				errorBody = ex.getResponseBody()
				fields.put("causeErrorBody", errorBody)
			}
			internalStatusCode = ex.getStatusCode()
     		fields.put("causeStatus", "" + ex.getStatusCode() + " (" + ex.getStatusText() + ')')
		}
		if (internalStatusCode == 503) {
			response.put("message", "A necessary downstream service is - possibly temporarily - unavailable. After a while try the request again! - Original error: " + ex.getResponseBody())
			response.put("errorCode", "SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE")
		} else {
			response.put("message", ex.getResponseBody())
			response.put("errorCode", "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR") // we can not identify a specific error
		}
	} else {
		response.put("message", "An unspecified error occurred. Please contact your administrator.")
        response.put("errorCode", "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR") // here we can not identify a specific error
	}
	response.put("fields",fields);
	if (!isNoneEmpty(errorBody)) {
		errorBody = '(empty)'
	}
	messageLog.addAttachmentAsString('ErrorBody', errorBody, "text/plain")
	messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('Status_business', 'failed')
	message.setBody(new JsonBuilder(response).toString())
	message.setHeader("Content-Type", "application/json")
	message.setHeader("CamelHttpResponseCode", 500)
	return message;
}
 
/**
* Checks if the provided object is a {@link CamelException} and has the specific fields 'responseBody', 'statusCode' and statusText'.
* @Param ex the object to test.
* @return TRUE if if the provided object is a {@link CamelException} and has the specific fields described above.
*/ 
def boolean isSpecificCamelException(Object ex, MessageLog messageLog) {
	if (ex instanceof CamelException) {
		Class exClass = ex.getClass()
		try {
			// following fields has to be exist:
			exClass.getMethod('getResponseBody', null)
			exClass.getMethod('getStatusCode', null)
			exClass.getMethod('getStatusText', null)
		} catch (NoSuchMethodException|SecurityException e) {
			messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('NoSuchMethod', e.toString())
			return false;
		}
		return true;
	}
	messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('NoCamelException', ex.getClass().getName())
	return false;
}
 
def boolean isNoneEmpty(Object o) {
	if (o instanceof CharSequence) {
		return o != null && o.length() > 0
	} else if (o instanceof Collection) {
		return o != null && !o.isEmpty()
	}
	return o != null;
}
 ​

 

 

 

//*groovy skript SAP Message
package com.scco.crm.update_erp_debtor
 
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.msglog.MessageLog
import com.sap.it.api.msglog.MessageLogFactory
 
import groovy.json.JsonBuilder
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper
 
import java.util.concurrent.ThreadLocalRandom
import java.util.HashMap
 
def Message processData(Message message) {
	def MessageLog messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
	String bodyAsString = message.getBody(String.class)
 
	messageLog.addAttachmentAsString('ERP-Update_ResponseBody', bodyAsString, "text/plain")
 
	return message;
}

entry.title: Re: Email Notes are standard feature available in SAP (XK01/XD01) but are not available in MDG UI. M
entry.content:
!!!Attention!!!

Step 6 creates a severe issue / bug in your custom code. It states to use CL_MDG_BS_BP_HANDLER as parent for your custom handler class. This is not correct. 

SAP MDG recommends using SAP MDG with S/4HANA 2023 which supports the address and communication data remarks out of the box. 

Best regards

Michael

entry.title: Re: Customizing MB11 Tansaction
entry.content:
Hello @ChiaraFxxx1 

I don't think you can customize MB11 that way without changing SAP standard screens. 

Let me say, that if you need to mass post goods movement from an Excel spreadsheet, it means that some of the work is done outside of the system, as someone needs to prepare the spreadsheet. That is not the way SAP is meant to be used. Instead, integrated processes should be planned and executed end to end in the system. If they were, you would not need to import goods movements from spreadsheets. 

If you really need to import goods movements from spreadsheets, you'd be much better off with a simple LSMW scenario posting material documents with BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE. Such a scenario is easy to build and can be utilized by end users - been there, done that.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP WM inbound delivery post error"Post the HUs for g...
entry.content:
Hello @NurlanJ 

Check the note 2077084 - Error HUFUNCTIONS 254 when posting the goods receipt for a 311 movement

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite OData V4 receiving format JSON
entry.content:
Hello,

In the SAP Public Cloud environment, especially when dealing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the system often expects data to be exchanged in XML format via APIs, particularly for standard communication scenarios like SAP_COM_0109. This explains why SAP Note 3266527 mentions the use of XML.

Here is why you might be able to create a sales order using Postman with a JSON body: Postman allows you to send requests in different formats depending on the configuration of the API endpoint and the content-type header you specify. It's possible that when you tested with Postman, you configured the request with the appropriate headers to accept JSON, and the backend system for Postman was able to process JSON for that specific endpoint.

In the SAP iFlow scenario, if the standard communication arrangement SAP_COM_0109 is designed to accept XML, it won't accept JSON directly. However, one way to handle this is by using a JSON to XML conversion step in your iFlow. You can introduce a content modifier or a mapping step to convert the JSON payload to XML format before sending the request.

Therefore, I'd suggest you convert your JSON to XML according to the SAP Note 3266527.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Cash discount in debit memo accounting document
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, when a debit memo is created with reference to an invoice, the behavior you're observing may occur due to how the system handles cash discount calculations in such scenarios. With the debit memo condition type DCD2 triggered, it typically should include all the necessary details for cash discount calculations, similar to the non-referenced debit memos.

However, it's possible that for debit memos created with reference to an invoice, the calculated cash discount amount may not display explicitly in the line item details of the accounting document due to system logic prioritizing the original invoice details for consolidation purposes. The cash discount information like base amount, payment terms, and baseline date is included to ensure the conditions for cash discounts are met without explicitly populating the calculated discount in the accounting document.

This can be considered a standard behavior in these scenarios, especially when the primary objective is ensuring consistency and maintaining references to the original invoice's financial conditions rather than calculating a new discount separately.

Warm regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Story Filters - Single vs. Multiple Selection
entry.content: Is there a way to setDimensionFilter with script to one value but the rest available members to still be available for selection? Currently if i setDimensionFilter with script, i see only one value and the rest of the available members are gone...

entry.title: Re: Session disconnect time for apps on SAP public cloud...
entry.content:
Hello,

When using SAP Fiori Launchpad and its applications, sessions can indeed time out due to inactivity, which can result in the loss of unsaved work like filling invoices. For security reasons, a user should be signed out after a period of inactivity, but this behavior can be configured.

Here's how you can manage and configure session timeouts for SAP Fiori Launchpad:

1. **Session Timeout Parameters**:
* `SESSION_TIMEOUT_INTERVAL_IN_MINUTES`: This parameter defines the timeout period for the security session in minutes. If set to `-1`, it disables the automatic sign-out. The default setting is disabled.
* `SESSION_TIMEOUT_REMINDER_IN_MINUTES`: This parameter sets the time in minutes before session timeout when a popup warning will be displayed to the user.
* `SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH`: This parameter works with the ABAP-server driven sign-out. It determines the time a user must be inactive before applications stop sending polling requests for data refresh.

2. **Stateful Applications Consideration**:
It's important to note that while you can set these parameters for the launchpad which controls stateless applications (SAPUI5-based), stateful applications like SAP GUI for HTML or Web Dynpro have their own session timeouts which might differ.

3. **Recommendations**:
- Keep the timeout values synchronized across your system to avoid inconsistencies.
- Consider user activity and usability when configuring these timeouts to prevent disruptions in tasks like filling invoices.
- Inform users about the session timeout policies and remind them to save their work frequently.

By adjusting these settings through the "Manage Launchpad Settings" app, you can help minimize session timeout issues and protect unsaved work from being lost.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: The COEP voucher corresponds only to value type 11 (11 actual statistics) with data, but value type
entry.content: The COEP voucher corresponds only to value type 11 (11 actual statistics) with data, but value type 04 (04 actual) has no data

entry.title: Re: Error when adding Activity Type to Operation on Tasklist
entry.content: this was the right answer for me. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP DM Inspection results not getting reflected in SAP ERP
entry.content:
Hello Mr.Schulz ( @manfred_schulz ), we have created a support ticket yesterday after finding a collaboration link "COLLABORATION_SYNC_INSP_RESULT"configuration is empty in Workflow configuration, but not sure its only the reason.

Here is the ticket number : Case 1383420/2024 (P2) - Inspection Results Recording Worklow Problem

Best Regards,

Jijhendra

entry.title: Re: Individual number enquiry
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Manage Material Coverage app does not provide direct configuration options to change how segment details, such as sales order numbers, are displayed instead of internal numbers like inquiry (IN) or quotation (QT) numbers.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How can I maintain decimals in calculating Frequency...
entry.content:
Hello,

The dates in your first example were 01.08.2024 to 08.11.2024 (100 days), and the anticipated leasing rent was $9,156.00 USD.

The daily amount would be 91.56 USD if we divided 100 days by US$9,156.00.

Please specify in your example the unit pricing and if it is daily or monthly. Based on the aforementioned, we have adjusted the frequency invoice cycle to monthly and the amount reference to daily.

Does the unit price shown in the screenshot below represent the daily amount? or a sum every month.

entry.title: End of Service
entry.content:
Dears,

Kindly i need to write a PCR to calculate the end of service by checking the action type MASSN and based on the Reason MASSG will do different calculations.

If Reason is Pension then will check the years of service, for the 1st 5 years then system should pay him 1.5 for each year, for the remaining years after the 5 years should pay him 2 months for each service year.

Would you please let me know how to write it.

 

Kind Regards,

Lobna

entry.title: Re: Create New Controlling Area in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: @huny As per SAP , the standard configuration involves only one Controlling area. A controlling area may contain one or more company codes which can operate in different currencies, if required. Each controlling area has one common chart of accounts. Deviating accounts, which may be necessary for example for legal reasons in some countries, can be maintained using an alternative account number .

entry.title: Re: SE63 Object Not Found
entry.content:
Hello,

Firstbalte as said, priorly you need to check if your smartforms is activated or not,

Second step is to check the "Language Attributes". If the button Into selected language is active, enter in side it and add you expect language to translate.

Best regards

Issam

entry.title: How to create inbound api endpoint for SAP DMC
entry.content:
Consuming external api endpoints is very clear and represented online in many tutorials and topics. The thing thats not clear for me is, can I create an endpoint on the SAP DM side? So that an external system can send information to the DM system. How far did I get:

1. created a service key in BTP

2. Created a destination in BTP, it uses the data from the service key

3. now I'm stuck. I think i need to add a Service in the Manage Service registry but what do I put in the URL? 

4. I want to achieve that when the endpoint is called from the external system a PPD is triggered but how can I connect the service or whatever to the PPD?

 

What do I need to do to configure this flow?

entry.title: Re: theoretical question: a tiny portion of hdb00003/data...
entry.content: Recovering the lost 16kb data could be possible but complex and risky. It requires deep knowledge of the HANA database and the datavolume_0000.dat file. You could try to repair the file or use professional data recovery services. However, success isn't guaranteed and it can be costly. To prevent such situations, implement a robust backup and recovery strategy.

entry.title: Re: Custom query options field not available in OData V4 SAP CPI- Not able to pass client number
entry.content: I had a similiar problem regarding odata4 service "api_measuringpoint". So just using odatav2 was not an option as it is not available as v2. Usually i just pass the filter/query options stored in ${header.CamelHttpQuery} to odata parameter "Custom Query Parameters" available in V2 & V4. But this did not worked for v4. The filter options were not passed to the backend service. Now I just add "&${header.CamelHttpQuery}" to the parameter "Query Options" at the end of the select list. This works for me.

entry.title: Mismatch in CJI5/Table Commitment Values & PR value
entry.content:
Hi all,

Against a single PR 4 no of PO's were created and all are deleted except one PO. PO value is less than that of PR & WBS also having Budget. But system throwing error budget exceed which is of 4 times that of PR value. Pl suggest a suitable solution to resolve the issue.

entry.title: Re: LSMW issue for transaction CG02BD
entry.content:
I suppose you used BDC in your LSMW and not BAPI/IDOC, AFAIK 757907 - Batch input is not supported for EHS transactions 

Did you already look for BAPI such as

  • BAPI - BAPI_BUS1077_CREATE            EHS: Create Specification
  • BAPI - BAPI_BUS1077_CHANGE            EHS: Change Specification
  • BAPI - BAPI_BUS1077_GETDETAIL         EHS: Read Specification

entry.title: Re: Session disconnect time for apps on SAP public cloud...
entry.content:
Thank you for your response! I will indeed check these parameters and see if we can resolve the issue!

Best Regards,

Fujita

entry.title: Re: How can I maintain decimals in calculating Frequency...
entry.content: Thanks for your prompt reply. The unit prices are Yearly amount.

entry.title: B1i Error OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space (failed to allocate 206995144 bytes) (max heap: 4096 MB)
entry.content:
Hello, 
since a few days we have a problem/Technical Error in B1i (Version 1.22.39)

Message:
com.sap.b1i.xcellerator.XcelleratorException:
XCE001 Nested exception: com.sap.b1i.bizprocessor.BizProcException:
BPE001 Nested exception: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError:
Java heap space (failed to allocate 206.995.144 bytes) (max heap: 4096 MB)

I can't understand the Error... 206 MB <> 4096 MB ???
Do you need more details about our system for possible solutions?

Thank you for your help

Greetings
Markus

entry.title: Re: sys.statisticsserver_sendmail_dev sending alert throu...
entry.content:
Try defining your SP as 

 

PROCEDURE "MYSCHEMA"."MYPROC_NAME" ()

SQL SECURITY INVOKER
AS

BEGIN

Declare l_ok integer;

 

call sys.statisticsserver_sendmail_dev ('<your mail - server>', 25, 'sender@email.com',

'receiver@email.com', 'Heading', 'Body', :l_ok);

 

END

entry.title: Re: "Engineering change mgmt called with data that could...
entry.content:
Recently, I face this issue. The error CC092 is due to material master is activated with deletion flag.

You need to remove DEL-FLAG using MM06.

Prafulla Devhare

entry.title: Thank you all for your great contributions and a warm Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year
entry.content:
Liebe SAP DACH Academic Community,

sehr gerne teile ich hier die Weihnachtsgrüße des DACH Academic Board.

Auch ich wünsche Ihnen frohe Weihnachten und einen guten Start ins Jahr 2025.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen

Andre Biener

+++++++++++++++++

Liebe Community,

Im Namen des Academic Board DACH möchten wir uns ganz herzlich für die tolle Zusammenarbeit in diesem Jahr bedanken und Euch schon einmal eine frohe und besinnliche Weihnachtszeit wünschen und einen guten Wechsel in das neue Jahr mit all den möglichen und hoffentlich friedvollen Herausforderungen. 

Wir bedanken uns auch für Euer Vertrauen und unsere Wahl als Sprecher des Academic Board DACH bis 2026. Gemeinsam mit Euch konnte die SAP ACC 2024 in Wien zu einem großen Erfolg werden und wir konnten einmal mehr lernen, was wir zukünftig noch besser machen wollen. Danke für die nachsichtige Übergabe des Staffelstabes an uns und ein großes Dankeschön an die ausscheidenden Mitglieder. Das Board ist um neue Mitglieder aus unsren Kernbereichen gewachsen und wir sind inzwischen noch universitärer aufgestellt. Wir planen bereits intensiv die SAP ACC2025 mit Euch allen und wollen dabei Eure neuen Ideen umsetzen.

Großer Dank an dieser Stelle an Andre Biener für die unermüdliche Unterstützung und die geduldige Anleitung zu einer strukturierten Umsetzung unserer hochfliegenden Pläne.  

Fröhliche Weihnachten und einen guten Start ins Neue Jahr

Uta Mathis und Hans Scheruhn

 

Dear Community,

On behalf of the Academic Board DACH, we would like to thank you very much for the great cooperation this year and wish you a Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year with all the possible and hopefully peaceful challenges.

We would also like to thank you for your trust and our election as spokespersons for the Academic Board DACH until 2026. Together with you, SAP ACC 2024 in Vienna was a great success and we were once again able to learn what we want to do even better in the future. Thank you for your indulgent handover of the baton to us and a big thank you to the departing members. The Board has grown to include new members from our core areas and we are now even more university-oriented. We are already planning SAP ACC2025 intensively with all of you and want to implement your new ideas.

We would like to take this opportunity to thank Andre Biener for his tireless support and patient guidance in the structured implementation of our ambitious plans.

Merry Christmas and a happy  New Year

Uta Mathis and Hans Scheruhn

entry.title: Return Request Process Automation Issues
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying 2 cases but can't run the template properly, 

Cases 1: Add template from Store, the template in status releases and I am able to be deployed it, but when I start new Instance it's not send to my in box to approve document, and process status is running as below

Case 2: Create from template, I am able to update SDK & Agent Version to latest, but when I run test for Automation it's said that Step name [XXXXXX] already used by another step.

Please help to share your ideas or experiences to fix these issues above.

Thank you so much,

Regards,

Tam

entry.title: Re: How to cancel a Billing Document in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
I go Change Billing Document and Go to Menu  and choose cancel Billing Doc 

and add cancel billing date and reversal reason and Click execute

And Save.

Where can I check the reason for canceling the billing document?

entry.title: Re: Demystifying CI/CD Pipelines for SAP Cloud Integration Projects
entry.content:
Thank you @SurajitNeogi for the great work. I deployed your solution and is working fine. 

When using the iflow Upload Integration Contents from GitHub i was getting the error statusCode: 403. To solve it, i had to edit the iflow and set the Runtime Configuration - HTTP Session Reuse - On Integration Flow. That was causing an issue when authenticating the last call.

entry.title: Re: Content Migration from JAM Enterprise to SuccessFactors Work Zone
entry.content: Hi, what are the prerequisite in this migration process? How long it may take to migrate all the contents from JAM to WZ? Did you face any challenges in the migration process? What are the things to do as next steps after raising a migration ticket to SAP?

entry.title: Re: Transfer stock after UD.
entry.content: Please tell me which movement type can be debited. And how to set up the movement? I found information that can be configured via TQ07M

entry.title: Import Supplier Data and Account Information for Generic Ariba Variant
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are aware that the Supplier Data and the General ledger files can be automated from SAP ECC or S/4 HANA to SAP Ariba B&I through the Master data export program. But we have the backend System as non-SAP ERP and we have Ariba Invoice Management Solution (Not the B&I suite but only Ariba Invoice Management) as Generic Variant.

Can someone please confirm if we can import/Automate the Supplier Data and the Account (General Ledger) information from Non-SAP ERP System to Ariba Invoice Management provided we have SAP BTP as the middleware. Is this possible, was anyone able to perform the Batch Import of Supplier and Account information from Non-SAP ERP to Ariba Invoice Management/Ariba B&I?

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Re: Maximum number of concurrent unattended execution rea...
entry.content:
Dear Prasad,

Thank you for reaching out to us. It appears that out of the two agents, only one has been identified as unattended. Could you please enable both agents, set their mode to unattended, and trigger the process again?

If the issue persists, kindly share a screenshot for further investigation.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Accounting Rules Engine (ARE) Powered by SAP PaPM Universal Model
entry.content:
Hi Edwin, 

Yes, you can send postings to S/4HANA using the so-called SOAP Service under Manage Connections, but Accounting Rules Engine is exclusively available in the Universal Model.

entry.title: Re: Easy Expert functionality does not contain specific c...
entry.content:
o Maria,


EasyExpert can only read "normal" characteristics, the source does not belong to those. So I am afraid you cannot use EasyExpert for this use case.

 

Best regards,

Ralph

Hi @Ganesh, @Ghs12 

have you update the values in the table T8JJ for your company code?

Regards, Maxim

entry.title: Re: CDS View in S/4Hana Cloud converting date format
entry.content: Hi Owen, I have created a CDS View with 3–4 fields related to dates (e.g., Posting Date, Journal Date, and Period Key). I want to change the default date format from DD-MM-YYYY to YYYYMM (with or without separators, e.g., / or -). Could you guide me on how to apply this customization either within the Custom CDS View creation or later in the Multi-Dimensional Analytical Cube? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Asset Accounting - Deprecation Below Zero
entry.content:
Dear all,
we have one question question in regards to Deprecation Below Zero.
The help portal describes basically exactly what we want to do
Asset Accounting - Base Method: Treatment of End of Depreciation at: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/4f3dde531ed3424de10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

For cost-accounting purposes we require the deprecation below zero. The help page indicates in the yellow marked part, that it is required that the depreciation area permits a negative net book value.
We struggle to identify in the cloud version how to configure the deprecation area so that it permits a negative book value.

Anyone has an idea if
a) the help description for the cloud version is correct and it is possible to configure the system as required and if yes how to achieve it
Or
b) help us in finding a solution/workaround if a) is not the case?

Thanks
Tobias

entry.title: Re: Freight Units Not Created When Using Document-Specifi...
entry.content:
I solved the issue by investigating the bgRFC-connection.

See SAP note 3099088

entry.title: Re: Async onInit - Route not trigerred
entry.content: Using "async" keyword for lifecycle hooks is not supported. Do not use async functions when implementing predefined lifecycle hooks, such as sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller#onInit. See https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/0cb44d7a147640a0890cefa5fd7c7f8e and https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/0cb44d7a147640a0890cefa5fd7c7f8e.html#loio0cb44d7a147640a0890cefa5fd7c7f8e/section_EHR

entry.title: Re: Executing desktop automation for multiple users
entry.content:
Hello Indhu,

Thank you for reaching out to us. I recommend saving the Excel file in a shared location and configuring the agent attribute accordingly. This should work for your use case.

If you encounter any further issues or need clarification, please share the details, and we will investigate further.

entry.title: Re: Integration Advisor – Support of xCBL Messages
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thanks for sharing . Informative blog . 

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: How to find input values for a BAPI. Can BAPI tcode b...
entry.content: Got the table to upload the objects

entry.title: Functional Buttons Disappear in SAP Screen Personas with Flavour Applied
entry.content:
• Description: I'm encountering an issue with SAP Screen Personas where the F1 and F2 buttons disappear when a flavour is applied. These buttons still function in the background but are not visible on the screen. All screen have missing button, for eg F1(log off) is present in all original screens but not in the flavour screen.
  • Steps to Reproduce:
    1. Open the RF screen in MOBGUI without flavour.
    2. Apply the flavour to the MOBGUI.
    3. Observe that the F1 and F2 buttons are no longer visible, although they still work in the background.

Additional Information:

  • SAP Screen Personas version: 

    Installed Software Component Versions

    Component

    Release

    SP-Level

    Support Package

    Description

    PERSONAS             

    300

    0018

                  SAPK-30018INPERSONAS             

    PERSONAS - SAP GUI PERSONALISATION

  • Screenshots: image 1(MOBGUI without Flavours)

Image 2(MOBGUI with Flavours)

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or have any suggestions on how to resolve this?

entry.title: Re: Error: Multiple identifiers not supported for OnDevic...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out to us. The on-device driver error you’re encountering typically occurs when the ID field is renamed. Renaming the ID field causes the resource configuration to break, as the rename does not update the identifier properties of the resource. Consequently, the resource still considers ID as its identifier, leading to issues down the line.

To avoid this, it may be worth considering that the ID field is clearly separated from other fields to prevent such conflicts. Let us know if further assistance is needed.

entry.title: Re: codepage: GUI_DOWNLOAD
entry.content:
DATA : l_encoding TYPE abap_encoding.

   l_encoding = '4310'.  
    CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download

   EXPORTING

codepage                = l_encoding

 

I confirm it's working and generated UTF-8 encoding file

entry.title: Re: Create New Controlling Area in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
@yogikkamal 

 it is not feasible to have multiple Controlling area. 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, the standard configuration involves only one Controlling area. A controlling area may contain one or more company codes which can operate in different currencies, if required.

Each controlling area has one common chart of accounts. Deviating accounts, which may be necessary for example for legal reasons in some countries, can be maintained using an alternative account number.

Learn more with documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6b39bd1d0e5e4099a5b65d835c29c696/50f1192d60274ed29eb0985b...

More detail information with all finance related collection of blog posts - https://blogs.sap.com/2018/08/16/intelligent-erp-update-sap-s4hana-cloud-for-finance-the-collection/

Thanks
Harvind Narayan

entry.title: Re: Error SAP_IPA_28014: Unable to process the requested Document Information Extraction
entry.content: Hello Ajay, Thank you for reaching out to us. Could you please provide more detailed information about the setup and the error you are experiencing? This will help us investigate the issue more effectively. Looking forward to your response.

entry.title: Re: GRDC Run Data Mapping, Import status error
entry.content: all the message type is successful for mapping

entry.title: Freight costs calculator in Secondary Distribution operations
entry.content:
Do you still plan trips without seeing your freight costs in your Secondary Distribution operations? Implico and SAP revolutionize freight planning with the Freight Cost Calculator!  

We've listened. We've created. We've delivered! As of 17 December 2024 (S4SCSD 4.0 FPS02), the IDM (Integrated Dispatch Management) module of SDM (Secondary Distribution Management) will have a Freight Cost Calculator as a product standard (S4SCSD 4.0 FPS02). The Freight Cost Calculator allows you to calculate freight cost during trip planning, to enable you to take educated decisions. 

 

Why This Matters:  

Traditional logistics planning leaves money on the table. Our new Freight Cost Calculator empowers dispatchers to: 

  • Optimize route selection beforehand 
  • Minimize transportation expenses 
  • Make data-driven decisions instantly 

Co-Creation Success: Developed based on customer feedback, this feature is the result of collaborative innovation with our customers and potential logistics partners. We heard your needs and transformed them into a powerful solution. 

Sneak Peek: Next level is coming soon. You will be able to compare route options and calculate the absolute best-buy scenarios for each trip. 

This isn't just a feature. It's a strategic tool designed with our customers' most pressing needs in mind. 

Interested in how IDM can revolutionize your Secondary Distribution operations? Learn more: https://www.implico.com/sap-idm/ 

Integrated Dispatch Management (IDM) is natively integrated into SAP S4SCSD.

entry.title: Re: 收货的分录中，存货科目的利润中心是判断什么自动带出的？
entry.content:
@LucyLu 
若同一個物料收貨入帳時需要區分A利潤中心與B利潤中心，那麼物料主檔的利潤中心就不能預設。
請問，這樣的情境要如何讓收貨分錄自動判斷要入A利潤中心還是B利潤中心?

entry.title: Reflecting on a Year of Innovation and Collaboration: A Message to the SAP Utilities Community
entry.content:
Dear SAP Energy & Utilities Community,

As we approach the end of another remarkable year, we would like to take a moment to reflect on our journey together. This year has been marked by incredible successes, collaborative efforts, and innovative solutions that have moved us forward.

We have summarized the highlights of the year in our video. Please take a look!

To you, our valued customers, partners, colleagues, and friends, we thank you for your support and commitment, which has laid the foundation for our success and without which none of this would have been possible.

If we take the SAP Energy and Utilities Conference in Seville as an example. We received such positive feedback there and left with lots of ideas and motivation to do our best to make this world a better place. It would be great if we could meet again this year in Rotterdam.

We are grateful for the trust you have placed in us and for the opportunity to work with such a passionate community 🤝

As we look to the new year, we are excited about the possibilities that lie ahead. Together, we will continue to drive change, take on new challenges, and create sustainable solutions for the future.

To stay up to date, visit our SAP Energy & Utilities Community Page.

Thank you for being an essential part of our community 🙏

We wish you Happy Holidays and a joyous New Year filled with health, happiness, and success 🎆

Yours sincerely,

SAP Energy & Utilities Team

entry.content:
Hello Satish,

Thanks for writing this blog. Really appreciate.

I am facing issue while reaching Odata via free trial account. However, while connecting to Odata with Oauth 2.0 authorization, i am getting "401 unauthorized" error although token being passed is correct.

 


Could you please advice?

BR,
Krushna Band

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay - Assistance Needed with Individual Goal...
entry.content:
Hi,

It seems that setting this option in XML: autoSyncP4PData="false" is my solution. I will run additional tests to confirm this.

entry.title: Re: Maintain Controlling Area in CBC - S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
@Wajed  In Cloud,  default content is always shipped with Standard Hierarchy 0001. Standard Hierarchy 0001 is the default structure and during cost center master data creation all cost centers get assigned to 0001.

The Standard Hierarchy 0001 is always fixed and no lower level cost center groups can be added. Standard Hierarchy 0001 is not editable in the Manage Cost Center Groups app by design. Reference the Create Cost Center and Cost Center Group (BNM) accelerator for additional information. Create Alternative Cost Center Hierarchy using the Manage Cost Center Group.

Customers can create an alternative cost center hierarchy as this is freely defined/maintained by customers and any lower level groups can be assigned. Each cost center must be specifically added to your defined alternative cost center hierarchy via Manage Cost Center Group app.

Thanks
Harvind Narayan

entry.title: Re: Standard change RFC auto status update once standard...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I am looking for below, currently we are using confirm action to move the RFC from implemented to complete state. But without any action how it can be done. SO that RFC is confirmed automatically whenever it reaches implemented status. Thanks once again.

entry.title: Email Notification for Final Approval in Flexible Workflow for PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using Flexible Workflow (top-level task: WS00800238) for Purchase Order (PO) approvals. The requirement is as follows: 

  1. When a PO is created, it goes to the frontend for multilevel approval via the Flexible Workflow.
  2. An email notification should be triggered only after the final approver approves the PO.
  3. Currently, the email is either not triggered or is being sent at intermediate approval levels, which is not desired.

Request for Assistance:
Could you provide detailed steps or configuration to:

  • Ensure that the email triggers only after the final approver approves the PO.
  • Validate any specific event settings, conditions, or prerequisites in the Flexible Workflow for this scenario.
  • Highlight any enhancements, BAdIs, or additional workflow configurations that need to be implemented for achieving this functionality.

Additional Details:

  • Flexible Workflow top-level task: WS00800238
  • SAP Version: [7.5 SP level 3]
  • Module: SAP MM / Workflow

Please advise on the necessary configurations or troubleshooting steps to fix this issue.

 

Any help is greatly appreciated.

Kind Regards,

Abhisekh

entry.title: After creating a new work schedule, while running time evaluation it is showing error.
entry.content:
Hi,

 Created a new work schedule and after assigning it to IT0007 and running time evaluation, it is showing this error 

Can someone explain on what can be done

entry.title: Re: SalesCloud Org Structure - Org Units: In which report...
entry.content: Hello Olgen, thanks for your answer. But unfortunately I have not found my OrgUnit records in the data source, nor are the ‘Address_line_1’ fields there.

entry.title: Re: Can I implement ICMR witohut Group reporting?
entry.content: Nice and informative blog @NaveenP1 . I had a question though- When it’s both GR and ICMR in scope, then where should we begin? Should we implement GR first then ICMR or vice versa. Please let me know your thoughts and please share if you have any reference material.  Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Error Message 15613 in MEINT for routing change
entry.content:
Hello Sergiy.

Thank You!

This solved our Issue at customer, after update SAP ME 15.3 including updating the DB driver for SAP ME (with the result, that the SAP MEINT Standard Interfaces CTC Wizard has to be executed again inclduing overwritting custom settings).

Regards

Matthias

entry.title: Re: Error Message 15613 in MEINT for routing change
entry.content:
Hello Sergiy.

Thank You!

This solved our Issue at customer, after update SAP ME 15.3 including updating the DB driver for SAP ME (with the result, that the SAP MEINT Standard Interfaces CTC Wizard has to be executed again inclduing overwritting custom settings).

Regards

Matthias

entry.title: Re: Error Message 15613 in MEINT for routing change
entry.content:
Hello Sergiy.

Thank You!

This solved our Issue at customer, after update SAP ME 15.3 including updating the DB driver for SAP ME (with the result, that the SAP MEINT Standard Interfaces CTC Wizard has to be executed again inclduing overwritting custom settings).

Regards

Matthias

entry.title: Creation of Pick-Up Freight Orders per Container for several Containers simultaneously
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have a question regarding the creation of pick-up freight orders for containers of an ocean freight booking.

Briefly about the general conditions: An Advanced TM (Embedded) is used and the release is SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS 2.

In an ocean freight booking, you have the option of selecting one or more container units or containers in the “Items” tab and then creating a corresponding freight order via Create --> Pick-up Freight Order.

If several containers are selected, they are all combined into one pick-up freight order. However, I would need one pick-up freight order per container. So you would have to select each container individually and create one such order. If there are only a few containers in an ocean freight booking, this is also a viable option.

However, I am faced with the challenge that an ocean freight booking often contains many containers. The solution described above is therefore not efficient for this scenario.

So my preference would be to select all containers and create one pick-up freight order for each of them at the same time. I have already researched this over a longer period of time and in various ways, but unfortunately I have not yet found a sensible way to map this.

I therefore hope that someone here has an idea of how to handle this scenario. It feels like this should not be a special case for which there is no solution in the standard system, but unfortunately I have not yet found one.

Thank you very much for your support!

entry.title: Re: Vendor missing inside PR created through MRP Run
entry.content:
Hi Caetano,

Thanks for your reply !!

Yes i checked and requirement date lies within the Validity date of Source list (6th Nov 2024 - 31.12.9999).

Also noticed that another PR of same Material displays Vendor in MD04, but not this PR. Even MRP has been executed today.

Regards,

Jeffrey

entry.title: Automatic Batch Determination in Consignment Issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I want to achieve automatic batch determination in consignment issue (CCIS order type) based on plant. For this, I have activated the batch determination for item category CIN. I have completed the config by doing the following:

1.Created a table with order type and plant, assigned it to strategy, strategy to procedure and lastly assigned the procedure to Sales Area and sales doc type. 

2. Maintained the record for the Sales doc type and plant

Now, while creating the order, i get the message "It was not possible to determine a batch"

Can you please help me determining a batch automatically in consignment issue. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Architecture Guide
entry.content: Tech Tonic blog  .

entry.title: HTTP Status 500 - internal server errror SAP BO BI 4.3
entry.content:
Hi,

I have been trying for days to install SBOP BI Platform Servers 4.3 SP04 Patch 900 and then SAP DATA SERVICES 4.3 SP3 WINDOWS. After the platform installation SAP BO BI is running. After the installation Data Services,  I get a HTTP 500 error and only the CMC of Data Services works. If I start wdeploy to recreate, then I can call the CMC of SAP BO BI again, but I can no longer open the CMC of Data Services and get an HTTP status 404 - Not Found error.

I just can't get both to work. With 4.1 and 4.2 I never had problems installing and opening both. Does anyone know what to do?

Thanks René

entry.title: Object Store Referece: Failed to update the instance ....not allowed to change the plan of reference
entry.content:
Hello! We are using object store reference instance in our mta like described here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/object-store-on-sap-btp-setting-up-instance-sharing-on-object-store/ba-p/13615448

In our mta we have something like the below with a valid uuid:

 

 

resources:
- name: objstore
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: objectstore
    service-name: objstore-ref
    service-plan: reference-instance
    config:
      referenced_instance_id: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxx-xxxxxxxxxx

 

 

 Deployment the first time works fine. When we do a second deployment, an warning is generated:

 

Updating service "objstore-ref" failed: Service broker error: Failed to update the instance xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxx-xxxxxxxxxx. It is not allowed to change the plan of a reference instance.
 
It's not a real error because the deployment continues but still weird.
We're not trying to update the plan. In BTP we can see this instance is set to reference-instance, just like in the mta.
 
Any idea why this is happening and how to avoid it?
 
Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: No authorization to access service group 'API_PURCHASEREQUISITION_2'
entry.content: @inesmartins How is the destination configured? If a technical user is used for authentication, does the user have the same authorizations as the user used in Postman?

entry.title: Re: How to change Control Account in SAP Business One Dur...
entry.content:
Hi @salah35 ,

  1. Balance Offset with JE - Post a JE choosing the BP account you're offsetting as both Debit and Credit. In this case, I'm moving a customer balance from 123010 to 123000. Obviously, you'll need to consider Debit and Credit differently when it comes to a Supplier:



  2. Internal Reconciliation - Reconcile the original invoice with the newly created JE:


  3. Post the payment to the new account - Now that you've reconciled the original invoice with the JE, the latter will be available for clearance. Preview the JE first and check if the control account is now the desired one.

This is just an example, do not forget to set dates and other parameters according to your accounting needs and principles, but that should do the job.

*** If this answer resolves your question, kindly consider marking it as accepted. This helps our community by highlighting useful solutions. Thank you! ***

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: SAP Application Interface Framework - Licensing
entry.content:
Hi Alexander,

What if we want to make use of automatic reprocessing of an /MMPUR INVREGQIN message via this mechanism: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_APPLICATION_INTERFACE_FRAMEWORK/1cefaed5b7a3471cb08564e54d5ba866/63bfba53d97def7ee10000000a4450e5.html 

Do we need a license for that?

Regards

Tom

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content: Dear @edeniz yes as part of the SAP Learning Hub partner subscription you have 4 attempts. This means 4 trials to pass an exam. Each trial that is passed or failed will be deducted from this entitlement. This means when you pass at the first trial the exam you can take other exams as well and when you pass as well at first trial you can in theory achieve 4 certifications with this subscription. Kind Regards, Suzanne
